When performing a code review, refer to the subject file in the .github/subject.md file to understand the context of the project and the requirements for the code. The subject file contains important information about the project, including security considerations and the command line interface (CLI) client features. Make sure to review the code with these requirements in mind and ensure that it adheres to the specified guidelines. You need also to take a look at the logging_client.h and the logging_server.h files to check if the features call the correct logging functions, and that a call to a log function is not missing. And finally, check if the response follow the protocol specified in the rfc_protocol.md file.
